g.text: READ FIRST - FIVE RULES THAT KEEP THIS SYSTEM ALIVE

g.desc: READ FIRST - FIVE RULES THAT KEEP THIS SYSTEM ALIVE

g.text: 1 BOTH BOARDS ARE 3.3 V AND NEITHER IS 5 V TOLERANT.

g.desc: 1 BOTH BOARDS ARE 3.3 V AND NEITHER IS 5 V TOLERANT.

g.text: The SAM3X's absolute maximum on an input pin is -0.3 V to

g.desc: The SAM3X's absolute maximum on an input pin is -0.3 V to

g.text: +4.0 V (SAM3X datasheet table 45-1). The Raspberry Pi

g.desc: +4.0 V (SAM3X datasheet table 45-1). The Raspberry Pi

g.text: documentation says plainly "Do not use 5V for 3.3V

g.desc: documentation says plainly "Do not use 5V for 3.3V

g.text: components", and the failure mode is latch-up, which shorts

g.desc: components", and the failure mode is latch-up, which shorts

g.text: the 3V3 rail inside the die. 5 V on a signal wire is a dead

g.desc: the 3V3 rail inside the die. 5 V on a signal wire is a dead

g.text: 2 THE THREE 5 V RAILS ARE NEVER TIED TOGETHER.

g.desc: 2 THE THREE 5 V RAILS ARE NEVER TIED TOGETHER.

g.text: PS1 feeds the Pi, PS2 feeds the Due, PS3 feeds the light,

g.desc: PS1 feeds the Pi, PS2 feeds the Due, PS3 feeds the light,

g.text: 3 ONLY THE GROUNDS ARE COMMON, AND THEY MEET AT ONE POINT.

g.desc: 3 ONLY THE GROUNDS ARE COMMON, AND THEY MEET AT ONE POINT.

g.text: Star ground at PS3(-). The load return - up to 1 A - must

g.desc: Star ground at PS3(-). The load return - up to 1 A - must

g.text: never share a wire with anything using it as a 0 V

g.desc: never share a wire with anything using it as a 0 V

g.text: 4 NO LOAD CURRENT THROUGH ANY I/O PIN.

g.desc: 4 NO LOAD CURRENT THROUGH ANY I/O PIN.

g.text: The light is switched by Q1's drain-source path. The SAM3X

g.desc: The light is switched by Q1's drain-source path. The SAM3X

g.text: pin drives a gate through 1 kohm and carries at most

g.desc: pin drives a gate through 1 kohm and carries at most

g.text: 3.3 mA, ever, and only for about a microsecond.

g.desc: 3.3 mA, ever, and only for about a microsecond.

g.text: 5 THE LIGHT IS OFF UNTIL FIRMWARE SAYS OTHERWISE.

g.desc: 5 THE LIGHT IS OFF UNTIL FIRMWARE SAYS OTHERWISE.

g.text: R2 makes OFF the power-on, in-reset, unprogrammed and

g.desc: R2 makes OFF the power-on, in-reset, unprogrammed and

g.text: post-fault state. That is a property of the copper, not of

g.desc: post-fault state. That is a property of the copper, not of

g.desc: design, because the SAM3X's reset state actively works

g.text: against it. Sheet 3 has the arithmetic.

g.desc: 2 power-and-ground the three supplies, the star ground,
